summary: Enhancement 8 — Global Tools (World Clock + Time Zone Converter + AI Best Meeting Time) tested end-to-end against the deployed backend and Expo web preview. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests PASSED including directory (>=25 zones), CRUD with autoseed (Atlanta/Manila/London), is_home mutex, invalid tz 400, converter math (ET 14:00 → Manila 02:00 next day, London 19:00, Tokyo 03:00 next day), and AI best-meeting via Claude (chosen_slot, candidates<=6, reasoning, base_date_utc). Frontend: /global hub shows World Clock tile (testID hub-world-clock); navigation to /global/world-clock works; 3 autoseeded clocks render; Add Paris flow via EditModal succeeds and row appears; reopening that row and clicking Delete + Yes,delete removes the row; Time Zone Converter with Atlanta source + 14:00 produced Manila 02:00 (Tue) and London 19:00 (Mon) result rows; AI Best Meeting Time with 3 participant pills clicked returned ai-result card with 'RECOMMENDED SLOT', UTC time, per-participant local times, and Claude reasoning + trade-offs (response in ~8s). Smoke check: hub-translator and hub-currency tiles also visible. One minor React-only warning: TZ_DIRECTORY uses 'America/New_York' for both 'New York' and 'Atlanta' which collides as a React list key in the EditModal select pills (console: 'Encountered two children with the same key, America/New_York').

react_native_issues.issue: Two entries share tz='America/New_York' ('New York' and 'Atlanta'). When rendered as select options in EditModal this triggers React 'two children with the same key, America/New_York' warning on every render. Reference: /app/backend/world_clock.py:36-37 and /app/frontend/app/global/world-clock.tsx:117-120. Fix by keying options by index or label (or by combining label+tz).

action_items: (Minor) De-duplicate the React key for select pills in EditModal — either key by `${o.label}-${o.value}` or by index, or rename Atlanta's tz entry. The duplicate key warning fires on every render of the Add/Edit Clock modal because TZ_DIRECTORY has two entries with tz='America/New_York'.
critical_code_review_comments: world_clock.py uses _candidate_slots that returns slot.score = in_hours_count even when len(participants)=0; safe because per_participant guards that case. OK.

best-meeting-time validates each participant's tz BEFORE the empty-list check, so 'participants:[]' falls through to the empty-list 400 only when the loop is empty — both invalid-tz and empty-list correctly return 400 (tests confirm).
TZ_DIRECTORY duplicate tz ('New York' & 'Atlanta' both = America/New_York) — see react_native_issues entry.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests use the existing test1@plos.app account; autoseed adds Atlanta/Manila/London on first /clocks call. Tokyo TEST clock was created then deleted by tests. Paris clock created/deleted by frontend run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Enhancement 8 acceptance criteria are met. The one minor non-functional warning (duplicate React key for America/New_York in EditModal options) does not break any flow but is logged on every modal render. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_world_clock.py is reusable as regression.
